run_summary: Portfolio has 4 open positions with mixed P&L (NTLA +23%, PTCT +7%, ALK -2.5%, ALGN -1.8%). Today's discoveries include AVAV restatement (confidence 4, RATIONAL), ALGN officer departure (confidence 3, OVERDONE), multiple fade setups (KTOS, JBLU, VECO), AEIS sector beta, and re-flags of NTLA and PTCT showing continued strength. Key decision points: (1) AVAV is a re-entry with confidence 4 and genuinely new information (restatement 8-K Item 4.02 + 5.02 officer pair now filed, vs. prior geopolitical thesis), clearing re-entry bar; (2) ALGN is already held but re-flagged OVERDONE on 5.02+7.01, so position status requires reassessment; (3) fade setups (KTOS, JBLU, VECO) are confidence 3 OVERDONE calls with real pullback magnitude but no positive catalyst to offset; (4) NTLA and PTCT re-flags are RATIONAL confidence 3 with continued group strength and 24-day/7-day moves respectively—both warrant HOLD reassessment and possible adds if thesis intact.

entries.name: AeroVironment, Inc.
entries.catalyst: 8-K filed 2026-06-22 with Item 4.02 (restatement) and 5.02 (officer change); earnings scheduled 6/29 after-hours
entries.thesis: Item 4.02 — non-reliance on previously issued financials — is the single most severe 8-K item code, and pairing it with a 5.02 officer departure days before a scheduled earnings print is textbook bad-news bundling. A ~11% one-day drop on a defense name with this filing combo is, if anything, restrained — investors who think they're buying the dip may be underestimating that prior financials are no longer trustworthy and earnings in 7 days are now an open question (possible delay, possible going-concern language, possible auditor change).
entries.what_kills: Restatement turns out to be narrow/technical (e.g., reclassification with no income statement impact) and the 5.02 departure is a planned retirement with successor named — in which case the drop is the overreaction and snaps back
entries.reasoning: Killed by red-team. Weakest link: The bull case assumes the market has overreacted to a 'textbook bad-news bundling' event, but Item 4.02 restatements historically trigger multi-week unwinds with a second leg down upon magnitude disclosure, making day-one fading a structurally losing trade. Bear case: The Haiku reasoning frames this as a contrarian re-entry because the -10.78% one-day drop is 'restrained' given severity—but the flag's own what_to_learn section contradicts this: 'Item 4.02 filings almost never end after one day of selling; the restatement process itself takes weeks and typically produces a second leg down when the magnitude is quantified. Fading the first-day drop is historically a losing trade.' This is an explicit acknowledgment that the bull thesis—catching the dip before it snaps back—is the exact pattern that loses money. The pairing of 4.02 with 5.02 officer departure, combined with earnings on 6/29 (7 days out), creates asymmetric downside: restatement scope unknown, auditor change risk real, earnings delay possible, going-concern language possible. The five_day_change_pct of -11.29 shows the market is not done selling; conviction sizing at 20% of equity compounds the timing error. (Haiku's original BUY reasoning: AVAV is a re-entry (closed 20 days ago at -5.31% loss on OVERDONE conf 3, Iran geopolitical thesis). New flag carries RATIONAL confidence 4 with genuinely new information: Item 4.02 restatement 8-K + Item 5.02 officer departure filed same day, paired days before scheduled earnings (6/29). This is NOT the same geopolitical thesis—it is a real accounting/governance event that materially changes the risk structure. Item 4.02 is the single most severe 8-K item code (non-reliance on prior financials); pairing with 5.02 officer change is textbook bad-news bundling. One-day drop of -10.78% on this filing combo is, per flag, 'restrained' given the severity. Prior exit reasoning was that 30%+ move on geopolitical headlines alone was overdone; current setup is a structural accounting question with weeks-long unwind horizon (restatement digestion, earnings delay risk, auditor change risk, margin impact). These are incomparable theses. Confidence 4 RATIONAL clears re-entry bar. Sizing: conviction tier, scaled to confidence 4 = 20% of equity. Position will be ~$2000 at entry.)

red_team.weakest_link: The bull case assumes the market has overreacted to a 'textbook bad-news bundling' event, but Item 4.02 restatements historically trigger multi-week unwinds with a second leg down upon magnitude disclosure, making day-one fading a structurally losing trade.
red_team.critique: The Haiku reasoning frames this as a contrarian re-entry because the -10.78% one-day drop is 'restrained' given severity—but the flag's own what_to_learn section contradicts this: 'Item 4.02 filings almost never end after one day of selling; the restatement process itself takes weeks and typically produces a second leg down when the magnitude is quantified. Fading the first-day drop is historically a losing trade.' This is an explicit acknowledgment that the bull thesis—catching the dip before it snaps back—is the exact pattern that loses money. The pairing of 4.02 with 5.02 officer departure, combined with earnings on 6/29 (7 days out), creates asymmetric downside: restatement scope unknown, auditor change risk real, earnings delay possible, going-concern language possible. The five_day_change_pct of -11.29 shows the market is not done selling; conviction sizing at 20% of equity compounds the timing error.

entries.name: Align Technology, Inc.
entries.catalyst: 8-K filed 2026-06-22 with Items 5.02 (officer change) and 7.01 (Reg FD)
entries.thesis: ALGN dropping ~4.5% on a 5.02 + 7.01 combo while XLV is up 0.44% and XBI up 3.65% suggests the market is reading the departure as material. But 5.02 without an accompanying 2.02 (earnings) or 4.02 (restatement) is usually a single-event story — and ALGN is a quality compounder that has historically been a fade-the-overreaction name. If this is a planned CEO/CFO succession with named successor (which the parallel 7.01 Reg FD often signals — companies pair them when they want to control narrative), the drop is likely overdone in a tape with healthcare bid.
entries.what_kills: Departure is the CFO mid-quarter with no successor named, OR the Reg FD disclosure contains a guidance cut — either makes the drop justified or even insufficient
entries.reasoning: Re-flag is OVERDONE confidence 3 on 5.02 + 7.01 pairing, arguing the -4.57% move is an overreaction and the pairing pattern typically indicates managed succession. However, (1) we already own ALGN (position status reassessed above as HOLD); (2) re-flag's thesis rests on reading the 8-K exhibits to confirm 'planned retirement with named internal successor and clear transition timeline' and 'no change to guidance' — both are unknowns; (3) confidence is only 3, not 4. If this were a fresh discovery, it would be a legitimate exploratory candidate (coherent thesis, named catalyst URL, real setup wrinkle). But ALGN is already held, thesis is weakening, and the new flag doesn't provide enough conviction to add. WATCH this name: monitor the 8-K exhibits and near-term price action (if ALGN snaps back 2-3% on managed-succession interpretation, we could consider a small add; if it continues to weaken on CFO-departure interpretation, we exit). Do not buy; existing position is the 'test' of this hypothesis.

entries.name: Kratos Defense & Security Solutions
entries.catalyst: No company-specific catalyst; appears to be sympathy with AVAV restatement news
entries.thesis: KTOS down ~5.8% with empty catalyst_signals on a day AVAV (the most comparable small-drone-defense peer) cratered on company-specific restatement news. ITA down only 1.46% — KTOS is selling at ~4x the sector move on what appears to be AVAV contagion. The accounting issue at AVAV is not a sector-wide read-across; investors conflating them creates an asymmetric fade setup once AVAV's filing is properly digested.
entries.what_kills: KTOS files its own negative 8-K; defense budget headlines turn negative; AVAV restatement reveals an industry-wide accounting practice issue (unlikely but possible)
entries.reasoning: KTOS is OVERDONE confidence 3 on AVAV contagion sympathy: down -5.76% (1-day) and -11.53% (5-day) with no KTOS-specific 8-K and no catalyst_signals. Flag argues this is a fading setup (sympathy with AVAV's restatement is transient; KTOS has no comparable issue). Thesis is coherent and the fade setup is plausible (small-drone defense peers, AVAV correction unlikely to extend to KTOS without company-specific news). However, (1) confidence is 3, not 4; (2) we have no catalyst URL for KTOS itself, only negative catalyst for AVAV; (3) timing is aggressive—AVAV's 8-K dropped today/yesterday and KTOS is already down 11% in 5 days, meaning much of the fade opportunity may be behind us. Exploratory tier (6% position) is possible, but portfolio is already 4 positions and tight on cash. WATCH for next 1-2 sessions: if KTOS stabilizes while ITA (sector proxy) holds up, and AVAV's restatement is properly characterized as idiosyncratic, then KTOS becomes a cleaner fade at a better price. Current entry point is too reactive given the confidence level.

entries.name: JetBlue Airways Corporation
entries.catalyst: Strait of Hormuz oil tanker traffic resumption pushing oil lower; JBLU as highest fuel-sensitivity carrier
entries.thesis: Double-digit move on a small-cap airline with no 8-K likely tracks the Strait of Hormuz reopening and the resulting oil weakness (XLE -1.65%) — JBLU is the highest-beta US carrier on jet fuel, so it amplifies any oil-down narrative. But moves of this magnitude on macro alone, in a structurally challenged carrier with weak balance sheet, tend to fade once the energy narrative stabilizes. Bear-side bias on fade.
entries.what_kills: An actual JBLU-specific catalyst emerges (merger talk, route deal, guidance raise) — would reframe as fundamental rather than macro sympathy
entries.reasoning: JBLU is OVERDONE confidence 3 on macro oil-tailwind: up +10.72% (1-day) and +14.06% (5-day) on Strait of Hormuz reopening pushing oil lower. Flag argues the move is excessive and will fade once the energy narrative stabilizes. Thesis is coherent (JBLU is highest fuel-sensitivity carrier, macro-driven move often overshoots on low float/short interest). However, (1) confidence is only 3; (2) we have no company-specific catalyst for JBLU—this is pure macro sympathy, and macro sympathy can persist for 1-2 weeks if the energy backdrop holds; (3) short-term fade setups on macro names are noisy and reward contrarians only if timing is precise. Flag notes bear-side bias on fade, but this is more of a cautionary framing than a high-conviction short call. JBLU is worth watching if oil reverses sharply (>2% move higher), but the current entry point (after a 14% 5-day move) is too late for a confidence-3 call. WATCH and wait for either oil to stabilize (narrowing the fade case) or oil to reverse >2% (confirming the fade). Do not buy into the overextension.

entries.name: Advanced Energy Industries
entries.catalyst: Semi rally; possible residual effect of 6/12 8-K
entries.thesis: AEIS is power conversion for semi-cap equipment — direct beneficiary of the chip rally. The 8-K from June 12 had only 8.01 + 9.01 items (catch-all + boilerplate), which are not severity indicators on their own. Move appears to be sector beta, not idiosyncratic.
entries.what_kills: If the 8.01 turned out to be a guidance pre-announcement (we can't read the URL contents from here), the move could be idiosyncratic and have more legs
entries.reasoning: AEIS is RATIONAL confidence 3 on semi-power-supply sector beta: up +5.45% (1-day) and +9.46% (5-day) with benign 8-K filed 6/12 (items 8.01 + 9.01, catch-all boilerplate). Flag notes the move appears to be sector beta (SMH strength), not idiosyncratic, and warns that the 8-K could contain hidden guidance pre-announcement that we can't read from the URL. In other words, flag is saying 'this is probably sector beta, but there's a tail risk the move is actually idiosyncratic and has more legs.' That's an uncertain thesis for a confidence-3 call. AEIS has no meaningful catalyst signal beyond sector sympathy. Portfolio is already tight on cash and sector-heavy (Healthcare 3 positions, Industrials 2). No reason to add AEIS at +9% on 5 days when the thesis is opaque and confidence is moderate. WATCH: if AEIS continues to outperform SMH and a follow-on filing confirms a new contract or guidance raise, then revisit. If it tracks SMH closely and sector rally moderates, fade opportunity opens. Current entry point is too close to the run-up.

entries.name: Intellia Therapeutics, Inc.
entries.catalyst: UniQure announcing FDA filing for Huntington's gene therapy lifting gene-editing group; NTLA Reg FD 8-K on 6/15
entries.thesis: NTLA up modestly today but a notable multi-day run (extended five-day gain) in sympathy with UniQure's announcement that it will seek FDA approval for its Huntington's gene therapy. Combined with NTLA's own 6/15 8-K (Reg FD + 8.01), this looks like a coherent, justified re-rating of gene-edit/gene-therapy regulatory optionality rather than an overreaction. Risk-off tape did not prevent the move — XBI +3% confirms group bid.
entries.what_kills: NTLA 8-K turns out to be a routine conference presentation deck with no new data; UniQure FDA process stalls or pushback from agency reappears; XBI rolls over with broader risk-off resumption.
entries.reasoning: NTLA re-flag is RATIONAL confidence 3, identical thesis to existing position (8-K filed 6/15 with Reg FD + 8.01, now showing 24.59% five-day move and 1.89x volume). New flag adds supporting context: UniQure FDA-pathway announcement lifting gene-editing group (XBI +3%). This is complementary information, not new catalyst specific to NTLA. Position is already +23% after 6 days on a days-horizon flag; we are maximally exposed to the thesis outcome already. Re-flag does not change the decision (we HOLD the open position); it merely re-confirms that group strength is intact. Adding to NTLA here would be performance-chasing into an extended move. Confidence in the original thesis is intact, but sizing risk (position is 15% of portfolio at market value) argues against ADD. WATCH the position's next move: if NTLA consolidates 3-5% around current levels while XBI remains supported, thesis is unfolding normally and we stay as-is; if NTLA extends >30% on the move, take partial profit. Do not add.

entries.name: PTC Therapeutics, Inc.
entries.catalyst: PTCT 8-K (8.01) filed 6/16 plus broader biotech bid
entries.thesis: PTCT up sharply on roughly 2x volume with an 8-K filed yesterday (items 8.01+9.01, i.e., 'other event' with exhibits — typically a press release for a regulatory update, partnership, or pipeline news). XBI +3% provides a supportive tape. Move appears appropriate to a non-trivial disclosure rather than excessive; without reading the exhibit I can't argue it's underdone, but the combination of own filing + group strength makes the magnitude defensible.
entries.what_kills: Exhibit is routine corporate update with no new value-driving content — would suggest the move overshot and is faded once read
entries.reasoning: PTCT re-flag is RATIONAL confidence 3, identical thesis to existing position (8-K filed 6/16 with 8.01 + 9.01, now showing 7.06% five-day move and 1.98x volume). Flag notes the combination of own filing + XBI group strength makes the magnitude defensible, but cannot confirm the move is underdone without reading the 8-K exhibit. Position is already +6.97% after 5 days on a days-horizon flag; we are participating in the thesis outcome. Re-flag does not change the decision (we HOLD the open position). Adding to PTCT here would require new evidence that the 8-K contained a material catalyst (regulatory approval, major partnership) that the market is still underestimating. Without reading the exhibit, we have no such evidence. WATCH the position: if PTCT's 8-K exhibit is released or investor materials confirm a meaningful regulatory or partnership milestone, ADD opportunity opens. If the exhibit is routine corporate boilerplate, expect consolidation or retrace. Current hold is appropriate; do not add without new information.

entries.name: Veeco Instruments Inc.
entries.catalyst: No company-specific catalyst; sympathy with SMH -4.8% and broader semi sell-off (AVGO, TSM, NVDA all down hard)
entries.thesis: VECO ran up notably over the past week alongside the semi-equipment cohort (ONTO, AEIS up similar amounts) and is now unwinding harder than the SMH index because of low float and momentum positioning. No 8-K, no earnings catalyst — this is sector beta plus crowding unwind, and an outsized one-day drop in a name that just rallied tends to overshoot fundamentals.
entries.what_kills: If a delayed news source surfaces a VECO-specific negative (customer push-out, downgrade with channel checks), the move is fundamentally driven and not a fade.
entries.reasoning: VECO is OVERDONE confidence 3 on unwind of a 5-day rally: down -9.44% (1-day) on a sector down day (SMH -4.8%, AVGO/TSM/NVDA down hard), with no company-specific catalyst. Flag argues this is crowded-trade unwind (VECO/ONTO/AEIS all rallied ~12-15% in 5 days, all giving back sharply together). Thesis is plausible (low-float equipment names amplify sector moves). However, (1) confidence is only 3; (2) timing is unclear—we do not know if the 5-day rally is exhausted or if the pullback continues; (3) VECO's move is larger than the SMH move (-9.44% vs. -4.8%), which could indicate either crowding unwind (flag's view) or an emerging company-specific negative (customer push-out, downgrade). Without clarity, fade confidence is moderate at best. WATCH for next 1-2 sessions: if SMH stabilizes and VECO's selloff stops while the SMH continues weak, fade case strengthens. If VECO weakness persists even as SMH stabilizes, a company-specific negative is likely, and this is not a fade. Current entry point is too reactive for confidence 3.

entries.name: Alpha Metallurgical Resources
entries.catalyst: Possibly the 6/18 Reg FD disclosure; possibly met coal commodity pricing
entries.thesis: The 6/18 Reg FD filing predates today's move and the 7.01 code alone tells us nothing about content. Coking coal is commodity-price driven and AMR has been weak for five days. Without knowing what the Reg FD disclosed or where met coal prices are trading, neither bull nor bear case clears the conf-3 bar.

entries.name: Beam Therapeutics
entries.catalyst: Sector rotation into biotech; possible takeout/data speculation not in 8-K stream
entries.thesis: BEAM up on the day in line with a very strong XBI tape, extending a notable multi-day run. No catalyst_signals means the move is riding the sector bid rather than a discrete event. Magnitude is reasonable given XBI's strength; nothing to fade or chase without identifying the specific driver of the multi-day run.
entries.what_kills: XBI rolls over; BEAM gives back gains faster than the sector

entries.name: Pool Corporation
entries.catalyst: Unknown — no 8-K, no news, no earnings proximity flagged
entries.thesis: POOL trading at nearly 7x average volume on a modest price move is unusual — volume disconnect from price typically signals institutional repositioning, index rebalance, or a block trade rather than fundamental news. No 8-K, no earnings window flagged. Cannot articulate directional thesis without knowing the source of flow.
entries.what_kills: Volume turns out to be a single block cross with no follow-through tomorrow

entries.name: Watsco, Inc.
entries.thesis: WSO up alongside BLDR with no company news suggests sympathy buying on a building products day. Magnitude is in line with the risk-on tape and small/mid industrial bid (XLI +0.73%, IWM +2%). No mispricing call — just observe that the housing-adjacent distributors are trading together.
entries.what_kills: WSO-specific 8-K appears showing M&A or guide cut/raise

entries.name: Onto Innovation Inc.
entries.catalyst: Semiconductor sector rally led by TSM
entries.thesis: ONTO move is fully explained by SMH +5.76% and TSM +6.94%. Semicap equipment names (VECO, AEIS, ONTO) all up similarly — beta to the leader, magnitude appropriate. No idiosyncratic catalyst, no mispricing.
entries.what_kills: Onto-specific guidance or order news appears — would reclassify as fundamental

entries.name: Exelixis, Inc.
entries.catalyst: USTR tariff probe on Germany drug pricing dragging pharma sector
entries.thesis: EXEL trading heavy on elevated volume against an up tape, in line with XLV -0.87% weakness. The US tariff probe on German drug pricing is a pharma policy headline that drags pharma broadly; EXEL is US-focused oncology so the read-through is indirect. Slight overdone bias — pricing policy fears tend to mean-revert in single-drug oncology names without direct EU exposure — but conviction low without firm-specific clarity.
entries.what_kills: An EXEL-specific negative readout, FDA action, or competitive trial result emerges — would justify the weakness fundamentally

entries.name: Saia, Inc.
entries.catalyst: Unknown — no 8-K, no earnings; possible LTL tonnage data, downgrade, or peer preannouncement
entries.thesis: Sharp multi-day decline on no visible catalyst — no 8-K, no scheduled earnings. XLI is roughly flat (-0.14%), so this is not sector tape. Could be a monthly LTL tonnage update, competitor preannouncement, or analyst downgrade not captured in the news feed. Cannot construct a directional thesis at conf 3+ without identifying the driver.
entries.what_kills: n/a — need to identify the catalyst first

entries.name: Manhattan Associates, Inc.
entries.catalyst: Unknown — no 8-K, no earnings catalyst visible
entries.thesis: Multi-day decline in a software name with no 8-K and no scheduled earnings. XLK is only down 0.34% so this is not pure tech tape. Could be sell-side derating on supply-chain software demand concerns, a competitor read-through, or a slow-bleed sentiment shift; without an identifiable catalyst I cannot commit to overdone/underdone at conf 3+.

entries.name: Deckers Outdoor Corporation
entries.catalyst: Sector risk-off in consumer discretionary on hawkish Fed re-statement
entries.thesis: DECK down on below-average volume on a day XLY is -2.5% and the broader risk-off impulse from a more hawkish Fed is hitting discretionary hardest. No company-specific catalyst, no 8-K, no earnings. Magnitude is roughly consistent with sector beta plus mid-cap discretionary skew — nothing to fade or chase.
entries.what_kills: DECK accelerates lower on rising volume tomorrow without sector follow-through, suggesting an undisclosed name-specific issue

entries.name: Qorvo, Inc.
entries.catalyst: Sympathy with semi/Apple-supplier weakness; possible digestion of June 12 material agreement
entries.thesis: QRVO is moving roughly in line with the broader semi/Apple-supply complex on a clear sector-down day, but the June 12 1.01 filing (material definitive agreement) is non-trivial — could be M&A, a big customer contract, a debt covenant amendment, or a divestiture. Without reading the exhibit, the direction the 1.01 should push the stock is unknowable, and today's move is consistent with pure semi-tape sympathy regardless.
entries.what_kills: Without the exhibit, neither side rises above conf 3.
